---
name: frontloom-vue
description: Use this skill for Vue and Nuxt frontend tasks. Enforces idiomatic Vue 3 patterns including Composition API, script setup, strict typing with defineProps/defineEmits, composables, Nuxt server components, data fetching with useFetch/useAsyncData, and Nuxt conventions. Always use alongside frontloom-core-fe for baseline quality standards.
---

# Frontloom Vue

Apply this skill to all Vue and Nuxt work.
Use alongside `frontloom-core-fe` for baseline frontend quality standards.

## Goal

Produce Vue code that is idiomatic, type-safe, composable, and production-ready.
Follow Vue's reactivity mental model — not patterns borrowed from React or Angular.

## Principles

1. Composition API with `<script setup>` is the default. Options API only in legacy maintenance.
2. Reactivity is the core primitive — `ref`, `reactive`, `computed`, `watch` drive everything.
3. Composables are the reuse primitive — extract logic into `use*` functions, not mixins or HOCs.
4. Single-File Components (SFCs) colocate template, logic, and styling. Embrace this.
5. Nuxt auto-imports reduce boilerplate. Lean into the framework, don't fight it.
6. Props down, emits up. Provide/inject for dependency injection across subtrees.
7. Server Components and universal rendering are Nuxt defaults. Add `.client.vue` only when needed.
8. Error handling is structural — use `<NuxtErrorBoundary>` and `error.vue` pages.

---

## Component Patterns

### `<script setup>` by Default

Always use `<script setup>` for new components. It's more concise, has better TypeScript inference, and is the recommended style.

```vue
<!-- Correct -->
<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref } from "vue";

interface Props {
  title: string;
  count?: number;
}

const props = withDefaults(defineProps<Props>(), {
  count: 0,
});

const isExpanded = ref(false);
</script>

<template>
  <article>
    <h3>{{ props.title }}</h3>
    <p>Count: {{ props.count }}</p>
    <button @click="isExpanded = !isExpanded">
      {{ isExpanded ? "Collapse" : "Expand" }}
    </button>
  </article>
</template>
```

```vue
<!-- Avoid — Options API in new code -->
<script lang="ts">
import { defineComponent } from "vue";

export default defineComponent({
  props: {
    title: { type: String, required: true },
  },
  data() {
    return { isExpanded: false };
  },
});
</script>
```

### Props Typing with `defineProps`

Use type-based declaration for full TypeScript support.

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
// Type-based declaration (preferred)
interface UserCardProps {
  user: User;
  variant?: "compact" | "full";
  onSelect?: (userId: string) => void;
}

const props = withDefaults(defineProps<UserCardProps>(), {
  variant: "full",
});
</script>
```

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
// Complex defaults
interface FormProps {
  initialValues?: Record<string, string>;
  validationRules?: ValidationRule[];
}

const props = withDefaults(defineProps<FormProps>(), {
  initialValues: () => ({}),
  validationRules: () => [],
});
</script>
```

Rules:
- Use `withDefaults` for default values with type-based props.
- Object and array defaults must use factory functions: `() => ({})`.
- Never use runtime declaration (`{ type: String }`) in new `<script setup>` code — type-based gives better inference.

### Emits Typing with `defineEmits`

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
// Type-based emits
const emit = defineEmits<{
  select: [userId: string];
  update: [field: string, value: string];
  close: [];
}>();

function handleSelect(userId: string) {
  emit("select", userId);
}
</script>
```

### v-model with `defineModel`

Use `defineModel` (Vue 3.4+) for clean two-way binding.

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
// Simple v-model
const modelValue = defineModel<string>({ required: true });

// Named v-model
const title = defineModel<string>("title", { default: "" });
const isOpen = defineModel<boolean>("open", { default: false });
</script>

<template>
  <input :value="modelValue" @input="modelValue = ($event.target as HTMLInputElement).value" />
</template>
```

```vue
<!-- Parent usage -->
<SearchInput v-model="query" />
<Dialog v-model:open="isDialogOpen" v-model:title="dialogTitle" />
```

### Slots

Use named slots for composable component layouts.

```vue
<!-- Card.vue -->
<script setup lang="ts">
defineSlots<{
  default: () => any;
  header?: () => any;
  footer?: () => any;
}>();
</script>

<template>
  <section class="card">
    <header v-if="$slots.header" class="card-header">
      <slot name="header" />
    </header>
    <div class="card-body">
      <slot />
    </div>
    <footer v-if="$slots.footer" class="card-footer">
      <slot name="footer" />
    </footer>
  </section>
</template>
```

```vue
<!-- Usage -->
<Card>
  <template #header>
    <h2>Settings</h2>
  </template>

  <p>Main content goes here.</p>

  <template #footer>
    <button @click="save">Save</button>
  </template>
</Card>
```

### Scoped Slots

Use scoped slots when the child component provides data to the parent for rendering.

```vue
<!-- DataList.vue -->
<script setup lang="ts" generic="T">
interface Props {
  items: T[];
  keyExtractor: (item: T) => string;
}

defineProps<Props>();

defineSlots<{
  default: (props: { item: T; index: number }) => any;
  empty?: () => any;
}>();
</script>

<template>
  <ul v-if="items.length" role="list">
    <li v-for="(item, index) in items" :key="keyExtractor(item)">
      <slot :item="item" :index="index" />
    </li>
  </ul>
  <slot v-else name="empty">
    <p>No items.</p>
  </slot>
</template>
```

```vue
<!-- Usage -->
<DataList :items="users" :key-extractor="(u) => u.id">
  <template #default="{ item }">
    <UserCard :user="item" />
  </template>
  <template #empty>
    <EmptyState title="No users" description="Invite your team." />
  </template>
</DataList>
```

### Generic Components (Vue 3.3+)

```vue
<script setup lang="ts" generic="T extends { id: string }">
interface Props {
  items: T[];
  selected?: T;
}

const props = defineProps<Props>();
const emit = defineEmits<{
  select: [item: T];
}>();
</script>

<template>
  <ul role="listbox">
    <li
      v-for="item in items"
      :key="item.id"
      role="option"
      :aria-selected="selected?.id === item.id"
      @click="emit('select', item)"
    >
      <slot :item="item" />
    </li>
  </ul>
</template>
```

---

## Composition Over Configuration

Prefer composable components over prop-heavy ones — same as React, Vue's slot system makes this natural.

```vue
<!-- Avoid — configuration explosion -->
<Card
  :title="'Settings'"
  :subtitle="'Manage your account'"
  :icon="GearIcon"
  :footer-action="save"
  :bordered="true"
/>

<!-- Prefer — composable structure -->
<Card>
  <template #header>
    <GearIcon aria-hidden="true" />
    <h2>Settings</h2>
    <p>Manage your account</p>
  </template>

  <!-- content -->

  <template #footer>
    <button @click="save">Save</button>
  </template>
</Card>
```

---

## Error Handling

### Nuxt Error Boundary

```vue
<!-- Wrap independently failing sections -->
<template>
  <main>
    <DashboardHeader />

    <NuxtErrorBoundary>
      <DashboardMetrics />
      <template #error="{ error, clearError }">
        <ErrorCard :error="error.message" @retry="clearError" />
      </template>
    </NuxtErrorBoundary>

    <NuxtErrorBoundary>
      <RecentActivity />
      <template #error="{ error }">
        <p>Activity unavailable.</p>
      </template>
    </NuxtErrorBoundary>
  </main>
</template>
```

### Error Pages

```vue
<!-- error.vue (app root) — catches fatal errors -->
<script setup lang="ts">
import type { NuxtError } from "#app";

const props = defineProps<{
  error: NuxtError;
}>();

function handleClear() {
  clearError({ redirect: "/" });
}
</script>

<template>
  <div role="alert">
    <h1>{{ error.statusCode === 404 ? "Page not found" : "Something went wrong" }}</h1>
    <p>{{ error.message }}</p>
    <button @click="handleClear">Go home</button>
  </div>
</template>
```

### `onErrorCaptured`

Use in composables or layout components to catch and log errors from descendants.

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { onErrorCaptured } from "vue";

onErrorCaptured((error, instance, info) => {
  logError(error, { component: instance?.$options.name, info });
  return false; // Don't propagate
});
</script>
```

Rules:
- Place `<NuxtErrorBoundary>` around independently failing sections.
- Use `error.vue` for fatal/page-level errors.
- Log errors via `onErrorCaptured` or error boundary handlers.
- Never swallow errors silently.

---

## Reactivity

### `ref` vs `reactive`

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref, reactive, computed } from "vue";

// ref for primitives and values that get reassigned
const count = ref(0);
const query = ref("");
const isOpen = ref(false);

// reactive for objects where you mutate properties
const form = reactive({
  name: "",
  email: "",
  message: "",
});

// computed for derived state
const isFormValid = computed(() => {
  return form.name.length > 0 && form.email.includes("@");
});

// Don't — reactive for primitive (loses reactivity on reassign)
const count = reactive(0); // Wrong
</script>
```

Decision guide:
| Data | Use |
|---|---|
| Primitive (string, number, boolean) | `ref` |
| Object you reassign entirely | `ref` |
| Object you mutate properties on | `reactive` |
| Derived value | `computed` |
| Template ref (DOM element) | `ref` with `useTemplateRef` or `ref<HTMLElement>()` |

### `watch` vs `watchEffect`

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { ref, watch, watchEffect } from "vue";

const query = ref("");
const category = ref("all");

// watch — explicit sources, access old and new values
watch(query, (newQuery, oldQuery) => {
  if (newQuery !== oldQuery) {
    fetchResults(newQuery, category.value);
  }
});

// watch multiple sources
watch([query, category], ([newQuery, newCategory]) => {
  fetchResults(newQuery, newCategory);
});

// watchEffect — auto-tracks dependencies, runs immediately
watchEffect(() => {
  document.title = `Search: ${query.value}`;
});

// watchEffect with cleanup
watchEffect((onCleanup) => {
  const controller = new AbortController();
  fetchData(query.value, { signal: controller.signal });
  onCleanup(() => controller.abort());
});
</script>
```

Decision guide:
| Scenario | Use |
|---|---|
| React to specific value changes | `watch` |
| Need old and new values | `watch` |
| Auto-track all reactive deps | `watchEffect` |
| Side effect that runs immediately | `watchEffect` |
| Conditional watching | `watch` with `{ immediate: false }` |

### `shallowRef` for Large Objects

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { shallowRef, triggerRef } from "vue";

// Large array — only track reference changes, not deep mutations
const items = shallowRef<Item[]>([]);

// Replace entire array (triggers reactivity)
items.value = [...items.value, newItem];

// If you must mutate in-place, manually trigger
items.value.push(newItem);
triggerRef(items); // Notify watchers
</script>
```

Use `shallowRef` when:
- The data is large (1000+ items).
- You always replace the value, never deep-mutate.
- You need better performance for large lists or complex objects.

---

## Composables

### Rules

1. Name composables with `use` prefix: `useAuth`, `useForm`, `useMediaQuery`.
2. A composable is a function that uses Vue's Composition API internally.
3. Call composables at the top level of `<script setup>` — same timing rules as React hooks.
4. Return reactive values (`ref`, `computed`) and methods.

### Pattern

```ts
// composables/useMediaQuery.ts
import { ref, watchEffect, onUnmounted } from "vue";

export function useMediaQuery(query: string) {
  const matches = ref(false);

  watchEffect((onCleanup) => {
    const mediaQuery = window.matchMedia(query);
    matches.value = mediaQuery.matches;

    const handler = (e: MediaQueryListEvent) => {
      matches.value = e.matches;
    };

    mediaQuery.addEventListener("change", handler);
    onCleanup(() => mediaQuery.removeEventListener("change", handler));
  });

  return { matches };
}

// Usage
const { matches: isMobile } = useMediaQuery("(max-width: 768px)");
```

### Form Composable

```ts
// composables/useForm.ts
import { reactive, computed } from "vue";

interface UseFormOptions<T extends Record<string, unknown>> {
  initialValues: T;
  validate: (values: T) => Partial<Record<keyof T, string>>;
  onSubmit: (values: T) => Promise<void>;
}

export function useForm<T extends Record<string, unknown>>({
  initialValues,
  validate,
  onSubmit,
}: UseFormOptions<T>) {
  const values = reactive({ ...initialValues }) as T;
  const errors = reactive<Partial<Record<keyof T, string>>>({});
  const touched = reactive<Partial<Record<keyof T, boolean>>>({});
  const status = ref<"idle" | "submitting" | "submitted" | "error">("idle");

  const isValid = computed(() => {
    const currentErrors = validate(values);
    return Object.keys(currentErrors).length === 0;
  });

  function setFieldValue<K extends keyof T>(field: K, value: T[K]) {
    values[field] = value;
    touched[field] = true;
    const fieldErrors = validate(values);
    errors[field] = fieldErrors[field];
  }

  async function handleSubmit() {
    const currentErrors = validate(values);
    Object.assign(errors, currentErrors);

    if (Object.keys(currentErrors).length > 0) return;

    status.value = "submitting";
    try {
      await onSubmit(values);
      status.value = "submitted";
    } catch {
      status.value = "error";
    }
  }

  function reset() {
    Object.assign(values, initialValues);
    Object.keys(errors).forEach((k) => delete errors[k as keyof T]);
    Object.keys(touched).forEach((k) => delete touched[k as keyof T]);
    status.value = "idle";
  }

  return { values, errors, touched, status, isValid, setFieldValue, handleSubmit, reset };
}
```

### Composables from Nuxt

Nuxt auto-imports these — know when to use each:

| Composable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `useFetch` | Data fetching with SSR support and auto-caching |
| `useAsyncData` | Custom async logic with SSR support |
| `useLazyFetch` | Like `useFetch` but doesn't block navigation |
| `useState` | SSR-safe shared state across components |
| `useRoute` / `useRouter` | Route information and navigation |
| `useRuntimeConfig` | Access runtime configuration |
| `useHead` / `useSeoMeta` | Manage page head tags |
| `useNuxtApp` | Access Nuxt app instance |
| `useError` / `clearError` | Error handling |

---

## Data Fetching

### `useFetch` (Primary)

Use `useFetch` for most data fetching. It handles SSR, caching, and reactive refetching.

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
}

const { data: users, status, error, refresh } = await useFetch<User[]>("/api/users", {
  // Transform response
  transform: (response) => response.data,
});
</script>

<template>
  <section aria-busy="status === 'pending'">
    <div v-if="status === 'pending'">
      <UserListSkeleton />
    </div>

    <div v-else-if="error" role="alert">
      <p>{{ error.message }}</p>
      <button @click="refresh()">Try again</button>
    </div>

    <div v-else-if="!users?.length" role="status">
      <EmptyState title="No users" description="Invite your team to get started." />
    </div>

    <ul v-else role="list">
      <li v-for="user in users" :key="user.id">
        <UserCard :user="user" />
      </li>
    </ul>
  </section>
</template>
```

### Reactive Params

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
const route = useRoute();
const page = computed(() => Number(route.query.page) || 1);

// Refetches automatically when page changes
const { data: users, status } = await useFetch("/api/users", {
  query: { page },
  watch: [page],
});
</script>
```

### `useAsyncData` for Custom Logic

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
const { data: dashboard, status } = await useAsyncData("dashboard", async () => {
  // Parallel fetching
  const [metrics, activity, alerts] = await Promise.all([
    $fetch("/api/metrics"),
    $fetch("/api/activity"),
    $fetch("/api/alerts"),
  ]);

  return { metrics, activity, alerts };
});
</script>
```

### Lazy Fetching

Use `lazy` option or `useLazyFetch` when data shouldn't block navigation.

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
// Doesn't block navigation — page renders immediately with loading state
const { data: suggestions, status } = useLazyFetch("/api/suggestions");
</script>

<template>
  <aside>
    <template v-if="status === 'pending'">
      <SuggestionsSkeleton />
    </template>
    <template v-else-if="suggestions?.length">
      <SuggestionList :items="suggestions" />
    </template>
  </aside>
</template>
```

### Mutations with `$fetch`

Use `$fetch` directly for mutations (POST, PUT, DELETE). Refresh related data afterward.

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
const { data: users, refresh } = await useFetch<User[]>("/api/users");

const isSubmitting = ref(false);
const submitError = ref<string | null>(null);

async function createUser(formData: CreateUserInput) {
  isSubmitting.value = true;
  submitError.value = null;

  try {
    await $fetch("/api/users", {
      method: "POST",
      body: formData,
    });
    await refresh(); // Refresh the user list
  } catch (error) {
    submitError.value = error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Failed to create user";
  } finally {
    isSubmitting.value = false;
  }
}
</script>
```

### Caching and Deduplication

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
// useFetch deduplicates by key (URL + params)
// Two components fetching the same URL only trigger one request
const { data } = await useFetch("/api/config");

// Explicit key for custom deduplication
const { data } = await useAsyncData("user-profile", () => fetchUserProfile(userId));

// Clear cache and refetch
await refreshNuxtData("user-profile");

// Clear all cached data
await clearNuxtData();
</script>
```

---

## Nuxt Conventions

### File-Based Routing

```
pages/
├── index.vue              → /
├── about.vue              → /about
├── users/
│   ├── index.vue          → /users
│   └── [id].vue           → /users/:id
├── posts/
│   └── [...slug].vue      → /posts/* (catch-all)
└── admin/
    └── [[dashboard]].vue  → /admin or /admin/dashboard (optional param)
```

### Layouts

```vue
<!-- layouts/default.vue -->
<template>
  <div class="layout">
    <AppHeader />
    <nav aria-label="Main navigation">
      <MainNav />
    </nav>
    <main>
      <slot />
    </main>
    <AppFooter />
  </div>
</template>
```

```vue
<!-- layouts/auth.vue — minimal layout for login/signup -->
<template>
  <main class="auth-layout">
    <slot />
  </main>
</template>
```

```vue
<!-- pages/login.vue — use a specific layout -->
<script setup lang="ts">
definePageMeta({
  layout: "auth",
});
</script>
```

### Middleware

```ts
// middleware/auth.ts — named middleware
export default defineNuxtRouteMiddleware((to) => {
  const { loggedIn } = useUserSession();

  if (!loggedIn.value && to.path !== "/login") {
    return navigateTo("/login");
  }
});
```

```vue
<!-- Apply to a specific page -->
<script setup lang="ts">
definePageMeta({
  middleware: "auth",
});
</script>
```

```ts
// middleware/auth.global.ts — global middleware (runs on every route)
export default defineNuxtRouteMiddleware((to) => {
  const publicPages = ["/", "/login", "/signup", "/about"];
  if (publicPages.includes(to.path)) return;

  const { loggedIn } = useUserSession();
  if (!loggedIn.value) {
    return navigateTo("/login");
  }
});
```

### Server Routes (API)

```ts
// server/api/users.get.ts
export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
  const query = getQuery(event);
  const page = Number(query.page) || 1;

  const users = await db.user.findMany({
    skip: (page - 1) * 20,
    take: 20,
  });

  return { data: users, meta: { page } };
});

// server/api/users.post.ts
import { z } from "zod";

const CreateUserSchema = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(1),
  email: z.string().email(),
});

export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
  const body = await readValidatedBody(event, CreateUserSchema.parse);
  const user = await db.user.create({ data: body });
  return { data: user };
});
```

### SEO and Head Management

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
// Static metadata
useSeoMeta({
  title: "Users — Dashboard",
  description: "Manage your team members and their permissions.",
  ogTitle: "Users — Dashboard",
  ogDescription: "Manage your team members and their permissions.",
  ogImage: "/og/users.png",
});

// Dynamic metadata
const { data: user } = await useFetch(`/api/users/${route.params.id}`);

useSeoMeta({
  title: () => `${user.value?.name} — Profile`,
  description: () => `View ${user.value?.name}'s profile and activity.`,
});

// Structured data
useHead({
  script: [
    {
      type: "application/ld+json",
      innerHTML: JSON.stringify({
        "@context": "https://schema.org",
        "@type": "Person",
        name: user.value?.name,
      }),
    },
  ],
});
</script>
```

---

## State Management

### Decision Guide

| Scope | Solution |
|---|---|
| Local to one component | `ref`, `reactive` |
| Parent-child | Props + emits |
| Subtree injection | `provide` / `inject` |
| SSR-safe shared state | `useState` (Nuxt) |
| Complex global state | Pinia |
| Server state | `useFetch` / `useAsyncData` |
| URL state | Route query params |

### Provide / Inject

Use for dependency injection across component subtrees.

```vue
<!-- ThemeProvider.vue -->
<script setup lang="ts">
import { provide, ref } from "vue";

const theme = ref<"light" | "dark">("light");

function toggleTheme() {
  theme.value = theme.value === "light" ? "dark" : "light";
}

provide("theme", { theme, toggleTheme });
</script>

<template>
  <slot />
</template>
```

```ts
// composables/useTheme.ts — type-safe injection
import { inject } from "vue";
import type { Ref } from "vue";

interface ThemeContext {
  theme: Ref<"light" | "dark">;
  toggleTheme: () => void;
}

export function useTheme(): ThemeContext {
  const context = inject<ThemeContext>("theme");
  if (!context) {
    throw new Error("useTheme must be used within ThemeProvider");
  }
  return context;
}
```

### Pinia Store

```ts
// stores/auth.ts
import { defineStore } from "pinia";

interface AuthState {
  user: User | null;
  status: "idle" | "loading" | "authenticated" | "error";
}

export const useAuthStore = defineStore("auth", () => {
  const user = ref<User | null>(null);
  const status = ref<AuthState["status"]>("idle");

  const isAuthenticated = computed(() => status.value === "authenticated");
  const isAdmin = computed(() => user.value?.role === "admin");

  async function login(credentials: LoginCredentials) {
    status.value = "loading";
    try {
      const response = await $fetch<User>("/api/auth/login", {
        method: "POST",
        body: credentials,
      });
      user.value = response;
      status.value = "authenticated";
    } catch {
      status.value = "error";
      throw new Error("Login failed");
    }
  }

  function logout() {
    user.value = null;
    status.value = "idle";
    navigateTo("/login");
  }

  return { user, status, isAuthenticated, isAdmin, login, logout };
});
```

```vue
<!-- Usage in component -->
<script setup lang="ts">
const auth = useAuthStore();
</script>

<template>
  <div v-if="auth.isAuthenticated">
    <p>Welcome, {{ auth.user?.name }}</p>
    <button @click="auth.logout()">Log out</button>
  </div>
</template>
```

### Nuxt `useState` for SSR-Safe State

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
// SSR-safe — state is serialized from server to client
const counter = useState("counter", () => 0);

// Shared across components with the same key
const preferences = useState("preferences", () => ({
  theme: "light" as "light" | "dark",
  locale: "en",
}));
</script>
```

Use `useState` over `ref` when:
- The state must survive SSR hydration.
- The state is shared across components without prop drilling.
- You don't need the full power of Pinia.

---

## URL State

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
const route = useRoute();
const router = useRouter();

// Read from URL
const sort = computed(() => (route.query.sort as string) || "newest");
const category = computed(() => (route.query.category as string) || "");

// Write to URL
function setSort(value: string) {
  router.replace({
    query: {
      ...route.query,
      sort: value === "newest" ? undefined : value,
    },
  });
}

function setCategory(value: string) {
  router.replace({
    query: {
      ...route.query,
      category: value || undefined,
    },
  });
}
</script>
```

URL state is right for: filters, sort order, pagination, search queries, active tab.
Vue state is right for: hover, focus, animation, form field values before submission.

---

## Performance

### When to Optimize

Do not optimize prematurely. Optimize when:
- You can measure a real performance problem.
- Components re-render expensively on reactive changes.
- Large lists cause visible jank.

### `shallowRef` and `shallowReactive`

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { shallowRef } from "vue";

// Large array — only track reference changes
const items = shallowRef<Item[]>([]);

// To update: replace the entire value
items.value = [...items.value, newItem];
</script>
```

### `v-once` and `v-memo`

```vue
<template>
  <!-- Render once, never update -->
  <footer v-once>
    <p>&copy; 2026 Company Name</p>
  </footer>

  <!-- Only re-render when specific values change -->
  <div v-for="item in items" :key="item.id" v-memo="[item.id, item.selected]">
    <ExpensiveItemCard :item="item" />
  </div>
</template>
```

### Async Components

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
import { defineAsyncComponent } from "vue";

// Lazy load heavy components
const AdminPanel = defineAsyncComponent(() => import("./AdminPanel.vue"));
const ChartWidget = defineAsyncComponent({
  loader: () => import("./ChartWidget.vue"),
  loadingComponent: ChartSkeleton,
  errorComponent: ChartError,
  delay: 200,
  timeout: 10000,
});
</script>
```

### Bundle Awareness

```vue
<script setup lang="ts">
// In Nuxt — use .client.vue suffix for client-only components
// components/HeavyChart.client.vue — not included in server bundle

// Or use <ClientOnly>
</script>

<template>
  <ClientOnly>
    <HeavyInteractiveChart :data="chartData" />
    <template #fallback>
      <ChartSkeleton />
    </template>
  </ClientOnly>
</template>
```

---

## Testing Patterns

Follow the project's existing testing setup. These patterns apply to Vue with Vitest + Vue Test Utils / Testing Library.

### Component Testing

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/vue";
import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event";

test("submits the form with user input", async () => {
  const user = userEvent.setup();

  render(ContactForm);

  await user.type(screen.getByLabelText("Name"), "Jane");
  await user.type(screen.getByLabelText("Email"), "jane@example.com");
  await user.click(screen.getByRole("button", { name: "Submit" }));

  // Assert emitted event
  // or mock the submit handler
});
```

### Testing with Pinia

```ts
import { render, screen } from "@testing-library/vue";
import { createTestingPinia } from "@pinia/testing";

test("shows admin panel for admin users", () => {
  render(DashboardPage, {
    global: {
      plugins: [
        createTestingPinia({
          initialState: {
            auth: { user: { role: "admin" }, status: "authenticated" },
          },
        }),
      ],
    },
  });

  expect(screen.getByRole("region", { name: "Admin panel" })).toBeInTheDocument();
});
```

### Testing Composables

```ts
import { withSetup } from "../test-utils";

test("useMediaQuery tracks media query changes", () => {
  const [result] = withSetup(() => useMediaQuery("(max-width: 768px)"));

  expect(result.matches.value).toBe(false);
  // Trigger media query change via mock...
});
```

---

## Common Anti-Patterns to Avoid

### Do Not

- **Use Options API in new components.** Composition API with `<script setup>` is the standard.
- **Mutate props directly.** Use `emit` to notify the parent or use `defineModel`.
- **Use `this` in `<script setup>`.** There is no `this` — use refs and composables.
- **Deep-watch large objects unnecessarily.** Use `shallowRef` or specific `watch` targets.
- **Use `v-if` and `v-for` on the same element.** `v-if` takes priority, which is confusing. Use a wrapping `<template>`.
- **Register global components for everything.** Use auto-imports in Nuxt or explicit local imports.
- **Use Vuex in new projects.** Pinia is the official store. Vuex is legacy.
- **Use mixins.** Use composables for logic reuse.
- **Put everything in `.client.vue`.** Default to universal rendering. Add `.client.vue` only when the component needs browser APIs.
- **Use `any` to silence TypeScript.** Fix the type instead.
- **Fetch in `onMounted` manually.** Use `useFetch` or `useAsyncData` in Nuxt — they handle SSR correctly.
- **Ignore error boundaries.** Wrap independently failing sections with `<NuxtErrorBoundary>`.
- **Skip `key` on `v-for` or use array index.** Use a stable unique identifier.
- **Store server state in Pinia.** Use `useFetch`/`useAsyncData` for server state. Pinia is for client state.

---

## When To Read References

Read only the files that match the current task:

- `references/semantic-html.md`
  Use when creating or restructuring markup.
- `references/accessibility.md`
  Use when building forms, dialogs, navigation, tables, or interactive UI. Contains both React and Vue examples.
- `references/typescript-strict.md`
  Use when defining props, API data, component state, or utility types. Contains both React and Vue examples.
- `references/ui-states.md`
  Use when building pages, forms, async views, or mutation flows. Contains both React and Vue examples.
- `references/security.md`
  Use when handling user input, external content, links, or third-party integrations. Contains both React and Vue examples.
- `references/responsive.md`
  Use when building layouts, navigation, or any viewport-dependent UI.
- `references/testing-vue.md`
  Use when writing tests for Vue/Nuxt components, composables, or Pinia stores.
- `references/performance-vue.md`
  Use when optimizing Vue/Nuxt load times, bundle size, or runtime performance.
- `references/css-architecture.md`
  Use when making styling decisions, setting up theming, or choosing CSS patterns. Contains Vue scoped styles section.
- `references/review-checklist.md`
  Use before finalizing significant frontend changes.

## Boundaries

This skill provides Vue and Nuxt idioms.
For cross-framework standards (semantics, accessibility, typing, UX states), defer to `frontloom-core-fe`.
